segments.text: [Speaker Notes] Chapter 19, Opener, Gout, a disease of purine nucleotide metabolism. Uric acid, an oxidized purine, is rather insoluble, and its accumulation can cause it to crystallize (crystals shown here), often in joints of the large toe. This causes a painful arthritis.
